enonce.texte: Complete the following sentences using the correct universal pronoun: each, all, every, everyone, everything, everywhere.

questions.texte: ______ student in the class participated in the gender equality campaign.
______ the students were excited about the gender equality club.
We should respect ______ person regardless of their gender.
______ has the right to equal opportunities in society.
In our school, gender equality is promoted ______.
______ child in the community must be treated fairly, no matter their gender.
Gender discrimination is unacceptable and we must do ______ to stop it.

enonce.texte:
Read the following poem about gender equality and answer the questions below.

Equality for All

Every girl and every boy,
Deserves a life of peace and joy.
Everyone should have the right,
To learn and grow with all their might.

All the people, young and old,
Should be treated as we're told.
Everywhere we look around,
Equality must be found.

Each person has a role to play,
To make the world a better way.
Everyone can make a change,
For a world that's fair and strange.

questions.texte: What does the poet say every child deserves?
questions.reponse: The poet says every child deserves a life of peace and joy.

questions.texte: According to the poem, how should all people be treated?
questions.reponse: According to the poem, all people should be treated fairly and with equality.

questions.texte: Identify the universal pronouns used in the poem and list them.
questions.reponse: The universal pronouns used in the poem are: every, everyone, all, everywhere, each.

questions.texte: What message is the poet trying to convey about everyone's responsibility?
questions.reponse: The poet is conveying that each person has a responsibility to contribute to creating a world where equality exists everywhere. Everyone can make a positive change.

enonce.texte: Identify and correct the errors in the use of universal pronouns in the following sentences.

questions.texte: All student in the class participated in the gender equality activity.
questions.reponse: Corrected sentence: Every student in the class participated in the gender equality activity. (or 'All students')

questions.texte: Every people should have equal rights.
questions.reponse: Corrected sentence: All people should have equal rights. (or 'Every person')

questions.texte: Everyone have the right to participate in activities promoting gender equality.
questions.reponse: Corrected sentence: Everyone has the right to participate in activities promoting gender equality.

questions.texte: Everywhere in the community promote gender equality activities.
questions.reponse: Corrected sentence: Everywhere in the community, they promote gender equality activities. (or 'Gender equality activities are promoted everywhere in the community.')

intitule: Creating a Gender Equality Campaign Poster

enonce.texte:
You are the leader of a gender equality club in your school. Create a campaign poster to promote gender equality among students. Your poster should include:

  • A catchy title
  • At least three sentences using universal pronouns (each, every, all, everyone, everywhere)
  • A call to action

questions.texte: Design the campaign poster for gender equality.
questions.reponse:
GENDER EQUALITY: A RIGHT FOR ALL!

Every student deserves equal opportunities!

All voices must be heard, whether you are a boy or a girl.

Everyone can contribute to making our school fairer.

Everywhere we look, we should see respect for all genders.

Each one of us has the power to make a difference.

Join the Gender Equality Club today and be part of the change!

intitule: Writing a Poem on Gender Equality

enonce.texte:
Write a short poem (about 8-10 lines) promoting gender equality. In your poem, you must:

  • Use at least four different universal pronouns (each, every, all, everyone, everything, everywhere)
  • Highlight the importance of equality between all genders
  • Include a message of hope and change

questions.texte: Write the poem promoting gender equality.
questions.reponse:
A World of Equality

Every girl and every boy,
Deserves respect, not just a toy.
All the people, near and far,
Should be seen as who they are.

Everyone can make a stand,
For equality in our land.
Everywhere we look, we see,
The strength of unity.

Each voice matters, each life counts,
Together we will break the bounds!

enonce.texte:
You and your classmates are planning a gender equality awareness activity in your school. Write a dialogue (10-12 exchanges) between two students discussing the activity. In your dialogue, include:

  • Suggestions for the activity
  • At least three universal pronouns (each, every, all, everyone, everywhere)
  • Reasons why gender equality is important

questions.texte: Write the dialogue between the two students.
questions.reponse:
Marie: Hello, John! Have you heard about the gender equality activity we are planning?

John: Yes, I have. I think it's a great idea. Everyone in our school needs to understand why gender equality is important.

Marie: Exactly! Every student deserves the same opportunities, regardless of their gender.

John: I agree. What activities do you suggest we organize?

Marie: How about a debate competition where each student can express their views on gender equality?

John: That's a good idea. We could also organize a drama performance about gender discrimination.

Marie: Yes! And we should hang posters everywhere to raise awareness.

John: All students should be encouraged to participate in these activities.

Marie: Absolutely. When everyone contributes, we can make a real difference in our school community.
